titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the David Grant Noble Photographs and Papers
titlestmt.author: by Janet Lopes and Michael Rush

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday March 30, 2022 at 03:25

did.unittitle: David Grant Noble photographs and papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Photographs, circa 1940s-2017. II. Papers, 1950-2017.

bioghist.head: David Grant Noble
bioghist.p: David Grant Noble (born 1939) is an American photographer and writer whose focus is the history and archaeology of the American Southwest.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 59 (computer media): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 60 (microcassette): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The David Grant Noble Photographs and Papers is the physical peroperty of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from David Grant Noble on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2021, and gift of David Grant Noble, 2021.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David Grant Noble Photographs and Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: David Grant Noble, Photographs of Southwestern Cultural Landscapes (WA Photos Folio 142), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.
David Grant Noble, Photographs of Mohawk steelworkers, New York City (WA Photos 318), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.
David Grant Noble, Photographs of Ojibwa wild rice harvests (WA Photos 380), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.
David Grant Noble, Photographs of Ojibwa cemeteries and Ojibwa families (WA Photos 401), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.
David Grant Noble, Portfolio of photographs of American Indians (1988 Folio 79), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The David Grant Noble photographs and papers document Noble's work as a photographer and writer. Photographs consist of black and white prints, color prints, platinum prints, contact sheets, proofs, negatives, slides, transparencies, and related papers. Subjects include landscapes of the American Southwest, Vietnam, New York City, Taos Pueblo, cars, portraits, China, the Yucatán, Ecuador, and others. Papers consist of lectures, publications, professional correspondence, unpublished writings, journals, and publicity and reviews.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four sub-series: Southwest landscapes, 1977-2013; Photographic projects, circa 1940s-2013; Non-black and white photography, 1970-2017, undated; and Book projects, undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The photographs series consists of black and white prints, color prints, platinum prints, contact sheets, proofs, negatives, slides, transparencies, and related papers documenting David Grant Noble's work as a photographer. Subjects include landscapes of the American Southwest, Vietnam, New York City, Taos Pueblo, cars, portraits, China, the Yucatán, Ecuador, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Southwest landscapes consist of predominantly black and white prints documenting Southwest ruins and rock art, "Great Gallery" in Blair Canyon, Chaco Canyon, Pueblo Grande, and other southwest landscapes. Also includes some color proofs, negatives, contact sheets, and project documentation.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into the following projects or groups: Southweset ruins and rock art; "Great Gallery" in Barrier Canyon; Chelsea project; Chaco Canyon; Southwest landscapes; and Pueblo Grande project.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: 14 x 17" matted prints and 16 x 20" matted prints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 14 x 17" and 16 x 20" mounted, overmounted, and matted prints.

did.unittitle: 1. Four generations of an Apache family, Kinishba, Arizona
164. Nuestra Señora de Los Angeles de Porciumcala de los Pecos
175. Glyphs with grasses, San Cristobal, New Mexico, no. 1
261. Navajo road, San Juan Basin, New Mexico
269. Petrogylph, Galisteo, NM, Comanche Gap I

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 65 loose prints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 42 8x10" loose prints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 76 finished prints and proofs of Chaco Canyon ruins and rock art. The proofs were considered for publication in books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 29 11x14" and smaller prints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes silver photographs, proofs, and negatives for the Phoenix Arts Commission.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 63 11x14" prints, documents and letters relating to the project, Phoenix Arts Commission paperworks, and contracts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 68 sheets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 139 black and white images.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 67 black and white images.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 69 rolls of film, 6x7cm and 35mm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographic projects include prints, albums, negatives, and contact sheets documenting David Grant Noble's photography, ranging from childhood photographs to projects in Vietnam, New York City, indigenous communities outside the American Southwest, Taos Pueblo, China, the Yucatán, and Ecuador. Also includes portraits, figure studies, cars, and various other subjects.

did.unittitle: Early photographic prints, albums, and negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs taken with a Brownie camera when Noble was between 8 and 11 years old. Subjects include animals in the National Zoo in Washington, D. C., Camp Dudley, Le Rosey School in Switzerland, and the high school football team that his father coached.

did.unittitle: Early photographic prints, albums, and negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph albums of Noble's European trip to England, Scotland, Ireland, Holland, and France.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 46 black and white prints, mostly on 8x10 paper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 58 black and white prints, mostly on 8x10 paper. Subjects include people living on the streets, building fronts, abstract patterns, and Central Park. According to Noble in his own description, "I made most of these pictures when I was first discovering photography as an art form. I had a strong interest, initially, in patterns especially on building. Also of street people."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 34 photographs, mostly 8x10 work prints, never exhibited.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 14 11x14 prints. These are mostly work prints, never exhibited.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 14 prints of various sizes and seven 35mm film strips and proofs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 60 5x7, 8x10, and larger prints, plus four contact sheets. According to Noble, in describing the photographs, "The name of the elderly Hunt Chief, who is shown in some of these pictures, can be found in Elsie Clews Parson's 'Pueblo Indian Religion' (1939). He was the hunt priest in the '30s and continuing into the 70s. Alfonso Ortiz told me that when he died, a great wealth of traditional knowledge went with him."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 14 prints of derelict cars in the desert and jungle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 17 8x10 prints. Series of portraits of Noble, his wife, and various friends when Noble moved to Santa Fe. Some photographs are related to building Noble's adobe house with the help of friends and nephews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 36 portraits of various sizes, loose and matted. Includes portraits of musicians, Arroyo Hondo sweat-lodge bathers, and an Apache family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 9 matted, 18 loose prints, and 1 contact sheet of nude female models.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 18 11x14" prints in 16x20" mats, and one 16x20 unmatted print of courtyard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 20 11x14" prints, including proofs of which final prints were not made.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 94 8x10 loose prints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 53 5x7" loose proofs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 11 8x10" photographs and 6 contact sheets. Noble notes he and Ruth, his wife, spent several days as guests of Stephen and Marcie Porter. Ruth worked for their son, Eliot, preparing photographs for portfolios. Noble went to China with another of Porter's sons in 1982.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 10 11x14" prints and 12 test prints, some with notations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 10 8x10 prints, 32 4x6" color prints, 26 5x7" black and white prints, contact sheets, and negatives.

did.materialspec: Includes 126 matted and loose prints of various subjects including beach scenes, street portraits, and landscapes from places like New Mexico, Utah, Montana, Hawaii, New York, and Mexico.

Includes a total of 46 matted and loose prints of various subjects including Western landforms, landscapes from New Mexico, Maine, and Hawaii, beaches, cityscapes, and street portraits. Some prints were exhibited in the 1998 Witkin Gallery show. According to Noble's notes, most are from the period of time he was changing size and format and printing on Kodak Ektalure G paper, a warm-tone emulsion with a fine-grained texture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The non-black and white photography series consists of platinum work prints, notes, manuals and color prints, transparencies, and slides.

did.materialspec: Includes work prints, notes, and manuals.

Includes color photographic prints, 6x7 mm transparencies, and slides. Transparencies include ruins and images relating to magazine and book illustrations and covers. Slides are of Mohawk steelworkers in New York City. There is also documentation of building a straw bale house for a Navajo woman near Gallup.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Book projects subseries consists of black and white prints used in two books: Ancient Ruins of the Southwest: An Archaeological Guide and Pueblos, Villages, Forts, and Trails: A Guide to New Mexico's Past .

did.unittitle: Ancient Ruins of the Southwest: An Archaeological Guide , photographic prints
did.materialspec: Includes 110 5x7" and 8x10" black and white prints used for the first three editions of the guide.

did.unittitle: Pueblos, Villages, Forts, and Trails: A Guide to New Mexico's Past , photographic prints
did.materialspec: Includes 82 5x7" and 8x10" black and white prints.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three sub-series: Writings, 1950-2017; Journals, 1965-2013; and Publicity and reviews, 1971-2010.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 59 (computer media): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 60 (microcassette): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers consist of lectures, publications, professional correspondence, unpublished writings, journals, and publicity and reviews documenting the work of David Grant Noble.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Lectures, 1988-2016; Publications, 1950-2017; and Unpublished short stories, 1959-1967.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 59 (computer media): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 60 (microcassette): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lectures document Noble's work speaking, teaching, and guiding tours related to the history of the American Southwest.

did.materialspec: Includes papers relating to Noble's teaching of courses in New Mexico. Courses include photography and subjects relating to the Southwest, New Mexico, and Santa Fe history. Listed courses include: "Traders, Travelers, and Tourists: How the Outside World Changed New Mexico," "The Spanish-Colonial Frontier," "Historical Santa Fe," "Trails to Santa Fe," "Double Vision," "New Mexico Territorial History," and "The Camino Real."

did.unittitle: Books, photography, archaeology, and history lectures and articles
did.materialspec: Includes topics as Fort Union, Santa Fe Trail, Chimney Rock National Monument, Impact of the Santa Fe Trail, Zebulon Montgomery Pike Expedition, Ute origins and history, Mohawk Steelworkers, Goodnight-Loving Trail, popular writing for "Writing Anthropology," "Living the Ancient Southwest," The Repatriation of Adolph F. Bandelier, "Ancient Ruins and Rock Art of the Southwest," "Ancient Colorado: An Archaeological Perspective," and Spanish-Colonial Santa Fe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Noble was a tour guide and lecturer on a 12-day bus tour from Salt Lake City to Phoenix. Tour members were British, Irish, and Australian.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 59 (computer media): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 60 (microcassette): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publications consist of magazines and other publications containing work by David Grant Noble, sales and gift records, correspondence, contracts, and otehrs papers related to Noble's books, and other professional correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes about 32 magazines and publications containing photographs and articles by Noble. Includes issues of Discovery , an athropological journal of which Noble was editor.

did.unittitle: Ancient Ruins and Pueblos, Villages, Forts, and Trails guidebooks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, contracts, permissions, royalty statements, and computer media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 59 (computer media): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relating to various book and photography projects and photo galleries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Moments Together," "Pigeons," "Meguro," "The Secret Places of the Forest," "Miguel," "The Squat Thrust," "Didi," "Mike's Diner," "Badger," "The Thief," "An Afternoon Walk," untitled, "The Magic Key," "The Llama's Tale," "The Giraffe Hunter," "The Wedding," "The Return," "Three Tales," and a play.
Also includes two papers written by Noble as an undergraduate at Yale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Journals document Noble's travels in China, across the United States and Canada, Vietnam, and Ecuador.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes journals from China, Native American tribal lands, and Noble's bicycle trip in Canada's maritime provinces.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes journal of Noble's time in Maine with Eliot Porter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an original and later version of a memoir written for family and friends. It became a memoir component of "Saigon to Pleiku: A Counterintelligence Agent in Vietnam's Central Highlands, 1962-1963".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: According to Noble's notes, he was invited by Nik Millhouse, thr proprietor, to be guest artist at Hacienda Cusin in Ecuador for two weeks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publicity and revews consist of materials related to books and photography shows. According to Noble, he "stopped collecting this kind of material in the late 1990s, probably because I ceased having relationships with galleries and having exhibits. But there have been subsequent book reviews."
